name: Stephanie's Raisin Scones
recipeIngredient: 8 tablespoons (1 stick) cold unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, nutmeg, and salt. Mix well.
2. Add butter and cut in with a pastry blender or rub in with your fingers until the mixture resembles fine granules. Add raisins and sugar; toss to distribute evenly.
3. Beat egg yolk with buttermilk (or yogurt) in a measuring cup and whisk with a fork. Pour over flour mixture and stir with a fork until a soft dough forms.
4. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead 10-12 times. Divide dough into two halves, shape each into a ball, turn smooth side up, and pat into a 6-inch circle. Cut into 6 wedges without separating them.
5. In a small bowl, beat egg white with a fork until just broken up. Brush top of each scone with the egg white and sprinkle lightly with sugar. Using a pancake turner, carefully transfer both cut circles to an ungreased cookie sheet. If necessary, reshape so that the 6 wedges in each touch to prevent burning.
6. Bake 18-22 minutes or until medium brown. Cool on a wire rack. After 5 minutes, gently separate the wedges and loosely cover with a dish towel.
Note: Egg white and sugar can be added before freezing the unbaked scones.
Variation: Replace 1 cup all-purpose flour with 1 cup whole-wheat flour and add 1/2 cup rolled oats to the flour mixture. Omit nutmeg.
